summaries.80648: The issue is confirmed specific to Chrome on iOS (not Safari, not Firefox); Safari shows autofill contact info but not the full suggestion bar. Reproduced on stable and master. Status: stalled, no fix — a 2-year-old bump comment notes no resolution.
summaries.88543: Reproduced on stable and master. A contributor attempted a fix by merging hintStyle into inlineLabelStyle but it broke two existing tests. The fix was ultimately reverted via #90406 due to more image diff failures than expected. Status: closed then reopened as landed-but-reverted.
summaries.134170: A Flutter team member pinpointed the iOS root cause: a deliberate engine-level choice in `FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm` to restrict keyboard types for password fields. Android root cause was not identified; `flagForceAscii` was ruled out. A recent re-check confirms the issue still reproduces on latest master (3.40.x) on both iOS and Android, and also affects Hindi and other non-CJK input methods on Android. Status: stalled, no fix.
summaries.162898: Root cause identified: Safari does not allow the trackpad/floating-cursor mode when `input.type='password'` — this is a browser-level restriction that applies to any `new-password` autofill hint. A commenter verified this on the native MDN demo and concluded the behavior cannot be fixed without setting `type='text'`, which would break password manager autofill. Status: stalled, effectively a browser limitation on web/iOS.
summaries.104500: The reporter proposed a concrete code fix using `getBoxesForSelection` to scroll to the selection extent rather than the caret rect. A comment noted the fix only covers mouse-drag selection, not selection handle dragging, and outlined further changes needed in `TextSelectionOverlay` and `_TextFieldState._handleSelectionChanged`. PR #102992 was created and merged, fixing the desktop case, but the mobile case was not fixed. Status: partially fixed (desktop), still open for mobile.
summaries.140044: Reproduced on both latest stable and master on iOS 17. A team member (@hellohuanlin) was pinged and suggested it is related to #137666. The root cause is described as a native iOS 17 highlight/selection layer that persists on screen after the text field is removed or the keyboard is hidden. Status: triaged as P2, likely related to #137666, no fix.
summaries.161217: Reproduced on latest stable and master on macOS. The KeyUpEvent for the Key S is not fired after `getSaveLocation()` returns — only `KeyUpEvent` for Control Left is received. The issue is tagged for @dkwingsmt. Status: confirmed on macOS and Windows, awaiting fix.
summaries.54998: Root cause identified as the default directional focus key bindings in `app.dart` being enabled on macOS (and potentially Linux). The fix was to disable them at the top level and re-enable per-widget (e.g., ListView). Linux was noted to also use directional nav in some apps, Windows less so. Workarounds provided: wrap in `Shortcuts` with `Intent.doNothing`, or bind `DirectionalFocusIntent` to `DoNothingAction`. Status: partially addressed; the default bindings were intended to be removed but the issue remained open with stale labels.
summaries.181236: Reproduced on latest stable (3.38.7) and master. Linked as another instance of #137666. A commenter identified the native fix mechanism: `UIScrollView` sends a private `NSNotification` on scroll that causes UIKit to dismiss autocorrect, and Flutter does not replicate this. Status: open, awaiting engine/iOS fix.
summaries.126329: The candidate list positioning issue is specific to fcitx-based input methods (Sogou uses fcitx); iBus works normally on Ubuntu but intermittently fails (~10% of the time). The UOS (uniontech) OS with fcitx also reproduces. Confirmed the issue is in the fcitx/iBus layer (positioning logic is IME-side), not exclusively Flutter. Status: stalled, awaiting Linux team investigation.
summaries.95928: Reproduced on stable and master on physical devices (not emulators). The issue only triggers when long-pressing at the beginning/top of the text, not at the bottom. A question was raised whether it also reproduces with `SelectionArea` + `Text` instead of `SelectableText`. Status: stalled, no fix.
summaries.124458: Reproduced on web only (stable and master). `TextInput.finishAutofillContext` triggers a second `onFieldSubmitted` call on web. The issue was referred to @LongCatIsLooong for triage. Status: confirmed web-only bug, stalled.
summaries.68292: Discussion concluded that changing the `validator` return type would be a breaking API change. The suggestion to use a `Result<S,E>` type was acknowledged as interesting but too big for a small change. A recommendation was made to file a design doc and add this to the big-breaking-changes issue (#24722). Status: proposal stalled, no action taken.
summaries.10372: Confirmed still reproducing on stable and master. A commenter noted that native iOS Contacts app also does not show the previous/next buttons, suggesting it may not be universally expected. Related to #72625. Status: stalled, long-standing open issue.
summaries.107055: Reproduced on stable and master on iOS (physical device iPhone 7) and Android (Chrome). The issue is more pronounced on iOS. No root cause was identified. Status: stalled, no updates from team despite user pings.
summaries.104693: The issue is specific to Safari on iOS; Chrome on iOS does work for OTP autofill. A user reported it worked in Flutter 3.13.0 for Safari, but others still report it not working. Status: conflicting reports, appears partially resolved in 3.13.0 but may regress.
summaries.51244: Reproduced on master. Also confirmed on iOS simulator (clicking another field doesn't fire `onEditingComplete`), and on Linux and web. A workaround is to wrap with a `Focus` widget to intercept focus loss. A clarification was added that `onEditingComplete` is intended for overriding default focus-change behavior, while `onSubmitted` signals user completion. Status: stalled, open with stale labels.
summaries.131176: Reproduced on both iOS and Android on stable and master. A maintainer asked whether native apps on both platforms support `obscureText` with `textCapitalization`, implying it may be a platform behavior question rather than a Flutter bug. Status: needs investigation of native platform behavior, stalled.
summaries.129174: Reproduced on stable. The issue is specific to Safari on iOS physical devices and only occurs when `TextFieldTapRegion` is present; without it the behavior is normal. The issue was assigned to @mdebbar but the assignee was later removed due to no progress. Status: stalled, unassigned.
summaries.96756: Root cause identified: `TextInput.finishAutofillContext` is called several frames late during route transition animation, after the autofocused new field already has focus. No single-field commit API exists on Android (the Android `commit` API affects the entire Activity). Proposed fix: hook into route's `willPop` or listen to `Route.isCurrent`. Assigned to @LongCatIsLooong, later unassigned due to inactivity. Status: stalled, unassigned.
summaries.90368: The Option/Alt key shortcut is broken on macOS web (Chrome and Safari) but works on Windows web and on macOS desktop. A commenter found a workaround: use `CharacterActivator('å', alt: true)` instead of `SingleActivator` with `alt: true`, which works if the keyboard layout produces a modified character for that key. Status: partially worked around, not fixed.
summaries.48296: Root cause hypothesis: Samsung Android devices may not produce key events for media keys until an `InputConnection` is opened (which happens when a `TextField` is focused). Pixel devices always produce the events. Suggested to switch from deprecated `RawKeyboardListener` to `KeyboardListener`, though this likely doesn't fix the underlying issue. Status: needs a physical Samsung device to debug further, stalled.
summaries.105001: Reproduced on stable and master with an iPad + physical keyboard + Japanese IME. Root cause analysis by @LongCatIsLooong: a specific keyboard relies on undocumented `UITextInputController` behavior that temporarily shifts selected text to insert composing text at the string start; Flutter's `-[UITextInput insertText:]` doesn't replicate this hack and the fix could break other text interactions. Status: stalled, engine-level iOS issue.
summaries.71318: Reproduced across many Flutter versions up through 3.3.x. The cursor visually appears on the left during typing but the submitted value is correct. Multiple related RTL issues linked (#47745, #50098, #54099). Status: stalled, long-standing RTL/obscureText regression affecting Android and iOS.
summaries.73930: The issue on Oppo R17 (Android web) was confirmed by others including Oppo devices on stable 3.19.3. A separate reporter also could not reproduce on iPhone Safari. The original issue (Oppo R17-specific) could not be reliably reproduced by triagers without the device. Status: stalled, device-specific, no fix.
summaries.91561: A maintainer confirmed native iOS does hide the cursor when the keyboard is dismissed by scrolling, but not necessarily by tapping on non-tappable areas. A draft fix branch was created to only hide the keyboard (not unfocus) on iOS, but exact native behavior is complex. No PR was merged. Status: stalled, no fix.
summaries.78660: Visual order (left = left, right = right) traversal support is acknowledged as a planned feature but not yet implemented. A commenter noted the existing Intent/Action system uses a `forward` boolean that may need to be extended to support left/right explicitly. Status: stalled, planned but not implemented.
summaries.166386: Root cause is a timing race: when the first TextField loses focus, `TextInput.hide` is called, and the bottom sheet's autofocus fires while the keyboard is still being dismissed, causing the keyboard to immediately close. Confirmed Android-only; iOS works correctly. A workaround is to delay the focus request with `Future.delayed` or `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback`. Related to #129179 and #125565. Status: has workaround, open for proper fix.
summaries.183571: Reproduced on both latest stable and master on iOS. A contributor implemented a fix in the engine (`FlutterCodecs.mm`) on a fork and submitted to Gerrit mirrors; a team member redirected them to submit a PR against the monorepo at `engine/src/flutter/shell/platform/darwin/common/framework/Source/FlutterCodecs.mm`. A pre-triage note suggests it may be a bug in `UITextInput.deleteBackward` rather than a duplicate of #179727. Status: P1, has a proposed fix pending a properly submitted PR.
summaries.43328: A maintainer clarified the expected behavior: with `maxLines: null` and a long placeholder in a constrained container, the placeholder should expand to multiple lines. The issue affects both `CupertinoTextField` and `TextField`. The root cause may be in the engine's `ParagraphStyle` handling when `maxLines` is null. Status: stalled, long-standing proposal with no fix.
summaries.159443: A detailed example was provided showing that `optionsBuilder` is only re-evaluated on `TextEditingValue` changes, so options fetched asynchronously after focus never appear. A draft PR #171929 proposes a `RawAutocompleteController` approach (similar to `TextEditingController`) that avoids breaking the existing API by using a separate constructor. Status: has draft PR #171929, waiting for someone to finalize it.
summaries.81976: Reproduced on stable and master on iOS and Android. The issue is specific to a `CupertinoTextField` inside `CupertinoNavigationBar` with a `FocusNode`; moving the field to the body or using a Material AppBar does not reproduce. No further progress or fix was made. Status: stalled.
summaries.128323: Root cause identified in desktop triage: the IME candidate list is repositioned asynchronously and sometimes loses a race condition, causing it to appear at 0,0 for one frame before jumping to the correct position. Proposed fix: only reposition the candidate list when the user commits a character or the cursor moves to a new line, not after every keystroke. A web user also reports Chinese IME not following the cursor. Status: stalled, Windows-specific, proposed approach agreed upon but not implemented.
summaries.120339: Root cause: on iOS Safari web, tapping the `suffixIcon`/`prefixIcon` triggers a `done` action from the virtual keyboard before the tap is processed, causing `onFieldSubmitted` to fire. Confirmed specific to `suffixIcon`/`prefixIcon` in `InputDecoration`, iOS browser only. Assigned to @mdebbar, then unassigned due to inactivity. Status: stalled, unassigned, iOS web only.
